headline: Political Appointees in GLCs Earn RM680M Combined
context: A governance watchdog analysis found that 342 political appointees across 48 major GLCs and statutory bodies earned a combined RM680 million in directors' fees, salaries, and benefits in 2025, with many holding multiple concurrent board positions.

cards.text: 342 political appointees earn RM680 million from GLC boards: average RM1.99 million each for part-time oversight roles
cards.sub: 48 individuals hold 3 or more concurrent board seats, collecting RM150,000-RM400,000 per board annually

cards.h: GLC board fees grew 34% since 2022 while average worker wages in the same GLCs grew only 4.8%
cards.s: Directors' fees at Petronas, TNB, and MAHB average RM420,000 per board seat, more than 10x the median employee salary
cards.d: GLC board fees grew 34% since 2022 while average worker wages in the same GLCs grew only 4.8% Directors' fees at Petronas, TNB, and MAHB average RM420,000 per board seat, more than 10x the median employee salary

cards.h: 87% of political GLC appointees have no professional qualification in the industry their GLC operates in
cards.s: Former politicians and party officials appointed to energy, aviation, and technology boards they have no expertise in
cards.d: 87% of political GLC appointees have no professional qualification in the industry their GLC operates in Former politicians and party officials appointed to energy, aviation, and technology boards they have no expertise in

cards.h: GLC board appointment process has no public criteria, no competitive selection, and no published performance evaluation
cards.s: Same individual can sit on 5 GLC boards simultaneously, averaging 4 hours monthly per board in actual meeting time
cards.d: GLC board appointment process has no public criteria, no competitive selection, and no published performance evaluation Same individual can sit on 5 GLC boards simultaneously, averaging 4 hours monthly per board in actual meeting time

cards.text: Paying RM680 million to political appointees for 4 hours of monthly meetings isn't corporate governance. It's a political patronage system with a corporate wrapper.
Like paying a security guard CEO wages to stand in the lobby four hours a month. The cost is corporate, but the purpose is political.
cards.sub: Board fees for minimal oversight create a patronage pipeline disguised as corporate governance

cards.text: Should all GLC board appointments require open competitive selection with mandatory industry expertise and a 2-board maximum?

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
